questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Heapsort?
options: A) To find an element in a sorted array
B) To sort an array of elements
C) To search for an element in a sorted array
D) To merge two sorted arrays
questions.explanation: Heapsort is a comparison-based sorting algorithm that uses a binary heap data structure to sort an array of elements.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a balanced search tree?
options: A) All nodes have two children (left child and right child)
B) All nodes have at least two children (left child and right child)
C) The height of the tree is relatively small compared to the number of nodes
D) The tree is always a complete binary tree
questions.explanation: A balanced search tree is a tree in which the height of the two subtrees of every node differs by at most one.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of Heapify operation?
questions.explanation: Heapify is the process of maintaining the heap property. The time complexity of Heapify operation is O(log n).

questions.question: Which data structure is used in the Transform-And-Conquer algorithm?
questions.explanation: The Transform-And-Conquer algorithm uses a heap data structure to solve various problems.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of using a balanced search tree?
questions.explanation: The main advantage of using a balanced search tree is that it allows for efficient search and insertion operations with an average time complexity of O(log n).
